items.passage: Many mammals survive harsh winters by entering hibernation, a state of greatly reduced metabolic activity. During this period, their body temperature can drop close to that of their surroundings, and heart rates fall to only a few beats per minute. Animals such as ground squirrels rely on stored fat that has been accumulated through the warmer months. Hibernating individuals are vulnerable to disturbance, however, because waking up requires significant energy. Researchers continue to study these patterns to understand how cells can tolerate such extreme physiological conditions.

Air pollution describes the harmful substances that are released into the atmosphere through human or natural activity. Common sources include vehicle exhaust, industrial smoke, and the burning of agricultural waste in fields near growing cities. These pollutants are carried by wind across long distances and can affect regions far from their origin. Health researchers have shown that prolonged exposure damages the lungs and contributes to heart disease. Although emissions controls have helped in many countries, dense urban areas still report serious pollution problems.

Children gradually develop the ability to think about objects, language, and other people over the first decade of life. Early development is shaped by experiences that occur during play, conversation, and everyday problem solving. Researchers have observed that infants are sensitive to patterns from a very young age. Their understanding of cause and effect grows steadily as they interact with their environment in increasingly complex ways. Although timelines vary, the underlying sequence of stages appears to be remarkably similar across many cultures.

Medieval guilds were associations of skilled craftspeople that controlled the practice of specific trades across many European towns. Members of these groups were required to follow strict standards regarding the quality and price of their products. Apprentices typically trained under a master for several years before being allowed to work independently. The guilds also offered support to members who were ill or unable to work because of injury. Although their influence eventually declined, these organizations shaped the economic life of medieval cities for many centuries.

Volcanism describes the geological processes that bring molten rock from beneath the surface to the outside of the Earth. Most volcanoes are located along the boundaries where tectonic plates either move apart or collide with one another. Eruptions can be explosive or relatively gentle, depending on the chemistry and gas content of the rising magma. Ash and lava flows often reshape the landscape for many kilometers around the volcanic vent. However, volcanic soils can also be highly fertile, supporting farming communities in regions otherwise considered hazardous.

Dwarf planets are small celestial bodies that orbit the Sun but lack the size and gravity needed to clear other objects from their orbital paths. Pluto is perhaps the best known example, although several others have been identified in the outer regions of the solar system. Their surfaces are often covered with frozen methane, nitrogen, and water that reflect sunlight at low temperatures. Observations by recent spacecraft have revealed unexpectedly varied landscapes on some of these distant worlds. Although they are small, dwarf planets remain important targets for ongoing study of solar system origins.
